regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, a Heat Advisory remains in effect with southeast winds at 6 knots under partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting stable conditions for embarkation and shore excursions. Port Everglades will see similar conditions with southeast winds increasing to 9 knots and overcast skies, maintaining favorable operations with minimal rain impact expected. Looking ahead to the next 24 to 48 hours, Port Canaveral will experience easterly-southeast winds around 8 knots accompanied by light drizzle but still less than 10% rain probability, allowing most activities to proceed as planned. Tampa Bay should remain unaffected by significant weather changes during this period. Beyond 48 hours, Miami is forecast to encounter an uptick in easterly-southeast winds to 15 knots with continued light drizzle and less than 10% rain probability, which may require slight adjustments to outdoor shore excursions but should not disrupt port operations.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today, conditions in Nassau and surrounding ports including Freeport and Bimini feature east-southeast winds at 8 to 10 knots with clear to partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth port operations and shore excursions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will remain steady from the east-southeast at 11 knots with overcast skies expected, particularly impacting Grand Cayman, Key West, and Ocho Rios, though rain chances remain below 10%, posing no significant disruption. Beyond 48 hours, wind speeds will increase to 18 knots from the east-southeast accompanied by light drizzle in Nassau and nearby ports such as Berry Islands and Samaná, yet rain probability stays under 10%, indicating minimal impact on cruise schedules. Vessel operators should monitor the rising winds for potential effects on small craft handling during docking and tendering procedures.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, a Heat Advisory is in effect for San Juan with east winds at 16 knots and a 62% rain probability, where thunderstorms are expected to impact port operations and shore excursions. St. Thomas, St. Croix, and St. Maarten may also experience periods of thunderstorm activity today as easterly winds remain steady near 15-16 knots. Through the next 24 to 48 hours, east winds will persist at 16 knots with thunderstorms and a 54% rain probability continuing to affect San Juan, St. Kitts, and Antigua, potentially causing delays in embarkation and outdoor activities. Beyond 48 hours, winds will increase to 19 knots from the east with thunderstorms decreasing in coverage, as the rain probability drops to 22%, suggesting improving conditions for cruise operations across the eastern Caribbean. Passengers and crew should remain prepared for active weather, especially in San Juan, where heat and thunderstorm impacts are most pronounced.
regions.western-caribbean: Today in Cozumel, expect east-northeast winds around 9 knots with overcast skies and a 32% chance of thunderstorms that may affect shore excursions and port operations. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will remain steady from the east-northeast near 9 knots with thunderstorms decreasing to a 12% rain probability, primarily influencing Cozumel and Roatan. Cruise operations should monitor these conditions closely due to variable weather and heat impacts in these ports.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today, elevated east winds at 21 knots are affecting Aruba with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, which may create breezy conditions for embarkation and shore excursions but should not impact port operations significantly. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, sustained east winds near 21-22 knots will continue across Aruba, Curacao, and Bonaire, with overcast skies on Day 1 and isolated thunderstorms forecast on Day 2, both with less than 10% rain probability, allowing for cautious continuation of cruise activities. Cartagena is expected to experience similar wind conditions, though local weather details should be monitored for potential variations. Beyond 48 hours, east winds remain elevated at 22 knots with drizzle and a 26% rain probability in Aruba, suggesting a slight increase in moisture that may affect passenger comfort during outdoor excursions but should not disrupt port operations. Cruise vessels should maintain awareness of persistent east winds throughout the Southern Caribbean to ensure smooth maneuvering and passenger safety.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today, an east wind at 14 knots will bring partly cloudy skies with a 42% rain probability and drizzle in Barbados, which may affect embarkation and shore excursions there as well as in nearby ports like St. Lucia and Martinique. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, increasing east winds up to 16 knots will coincide with a 57% chance of rain showers, expected to impact port operations and outdoor activities in Dominica, Antigua, and St. Kitts. By day three, winds will ease slightly to 15 knots from the east with a 42% chance of drizzle, potentially affecting Grenada and St. Vincent’s shore excursions. Cruise vessels should anticipate variable wet conditions and adjust scheduling accordingly across the Lesser Antilles through this period.
regions.los-angeles: Today in the Los Angeles and San Pedro area, light northeasterly winds at 2 knots and clear skies support smooth port operations and uninterrupted shore excursions at Los Angeles, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island, with less than 10% rain probability. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, westerly winds will increase to 12 knots accompanied by overcast skies, especially impacting embarkation and disembarkation activities at San Pedro and Long Beach, though rain chances remain below 10%. By day two, winds shift to the west-southwest at 11 knots with partly cloudy conditions, maintaining favorable conditions for cruise operations at all ports including Catalina Island. Looking beyond 48 hours, winds will veer to the south-southeast at 10 knots with a return to clear skies and continued low rain probability, indicating stable conditions for sailing and port visits across the region.
regions.ensenada: Today, Ensenada is experiencing light southerly winds at 2 knots with foggy conditions and less than 10% rain probability, which may slightly reduce visibility for embarkation and shore excursions but should not significantly disrupt port operations. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will shift to the west-southwest at 9 knots while fog persists, affecting Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos; these conditions warrant caution during tender operations due to limited visibility. Beyond 48 hours, expect continued west-southwest winds at 9 knots with skies becoming overcast in the Ensenada area, maintaining less than 10% rain probability and stable conditions for cruise activities. Passengers and crew should anticipate consistent light winds and low precipitation chances throughout the period, with fog gradually giving way to overcast skies by day three.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today, Cabo San Lucas and nearby ports including San Jose del Cabo and La Paz will experience clear skies with light west-northwest winds at 7 knots and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursion activities. On July 8, an elevated heat day is forecast with the heat index reaching 104 degrees Fahrenheit, particularly impacting all three ports, while winds increase slightly from the west at 12 knots and skies remain partly cloudy with minimal rain threat. By July 9, conditions will remain stable with west winds at 10 knots, partly cloudy skies, and less than 10% rain probability, allowing normal port operations to continue. Passengers and crew are advised to stay hydrated and take heat precautions during outdoor activities on July 8 due to the elevated heat index. No significant changes are expected beyond this period, maintaining favorable conditions for cruise operations in the region.
regions.mazatlan: Today in the Mazatlan region, including the ports of Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata, easterly-northeast winds at 4 knots accompany drizzle with a 60% rain probability, leading to expected impacts on embarkation and shore excursions due to wet conditions. Thunderstorms are forecast with south winds at 7 knots and a sustained 60% rain probability, which may result in delays or operational adjustments at these ports. In the next 24 to 48 hours, thunderstorms will persist with winds shifting to south-southeast at 9 knots and a 56% rain probability, particularly affecting Topolobampo and Altata shore activities. Beyond 48 hours, thunderstorms continue with east-southeast winds at 6 knots and a 62% rain probability, maintaining the likelihood of wet weather disruptions for cruise operations across all three ports.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today, elevated heat with a heat index reaching 101 degrees poses a consideration for outdoor activities in Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala. Winds are light from the north-northeast at 4 knots with drizzle and a 44% rain probability, which may affect shore excursions and embarkation processes at all ports. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, sustained winds will shift to the east-southeast at 9 knots with continued thunderstorms and a 45% rain probability, particularly impacting Punta Mita and Puerto Vallarta operations. By day three, winds will moderate to 7 knots from the east-northeast with thunderstorms persisting at a 39% rain probability, suggesting ongoing caution for outdoor port activities in Yelapa and Chacala. Cruise vessels should prepare for intermittent weather disruptions and adjust schedules accordingly.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today, southeast winds at 8 knots and clear skies with less than 10% rain probability will support smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations in Barcelona, with shore excursions proceeding without weather-related interruptions. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, ports including Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, and Ibiza can expect light winds shifting from south-southeast to south-southwest at around 8 knots, accompanied by partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability, maintaining favorable conditions for cruise operations. By day three, winds will shift to a southerly direction near 9 knots with overcast skies but still less than 10% rain probability, which may reduce direct sunlight during shore activities in Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar without impacting port functionality. No significant weather changes or rain threats are forecast beyond 48 hours, allowing cruise schedules in this Western Mediterranean region to continue with minimal meteorological disruption.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, winds are steady from the south-southwest at 7 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability, supporting smooth embarkation and shore excursions in Naples and nearby ports such as Civitavecchia and Livorno. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect similar light winds around 8 knots shifting from south-southwest to southwest, with partly cloudy conditions and a continued less than 10% chance of rain, favorable for port operations in Genoa, La Spezia, and Naples. By day three, a west-southwest wind at 8 knots will bring overcast skies to the region including Sardinia and Corsica, though rain probability remains under 10%, posing no operational concerns. Cruise vessels can anticipate consistent moderate winds and dry conditions throughout the Central Mediterranean, ensuring stable sea conditions for navigation and onshore activities. No significant weather disruptions are forecast beyond 48 hours.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today, winds out of the north-northwest at 6 knots with clear skies and less than 10% rain probability will support smooth port operations and favorable conditions for shore excursions in Athens (Piraeus) and nearby ports such as Santorini and Mykonos. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, expect a shift to southerly winds at 7 knots with overcast skies but still less than 10% rain probability, which will affect Athens, Rhodes, and Izmir with predominantly dry conditions but reduced sunshine. Beyond 48 hours, northeasterly winds at 7 knots will accompany rain showers and a 12% rain probability, potentially influencing embarkation and disembarkation activities in Athens, Cyprus, and Haifa. Cruise vessels should anticipate minor adjustments in itinerary timing due to cloud cover and light precipitation chances during that period. Overall, stable conditions prevail today and tomorrow with increasing cloudiness and slight rain chances by the third day.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today, Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway will experience easterly-southeast winds around 5 to 6 knots with a 46% chance of rain showers, which may affect shore excursions and outdoor activities in these ports. Visibility should remain good under partly cloudy skies despite the elevated rain probability. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, rain is expected to increase with drizzle and a 60% rain probability in Juneau, likely impacting port operations and passenger movement; similar conditions may also extend to Ketchikan and Skagway as easterly winds strengthen to around 8 knots. Beyond 48 hours, a 44% chance of drizzle with winds near 6 knots will continue to influence the Inside Passage, requiring ongoing attention to slick surfaces during shore excursions. Seattle and Vancouver remain clear of notable weather impacts during this period.
